title: Magonia corylifolia

The genus Magonia is part of the family Annonaceae and is native to tropical regions. Magonia corylifolia is also known by its synonyms, including Annona corylifolia. The exact classification and details of Magonia corylifolia may vary based on different botanical sources.

intro: Magonia corylifolia, also known as Magaz, is a plant species belonging to the Sapindaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Magonia corylifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Magonia corylifolia is commonly used to prepare herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and powder, often in combination with other herbs to treat conditions like bronchitis, cough, and fever.
